title: Gibbs Free Energy and Chemical Spontaneity
content: The Gibbs free energy G = H − TS determines spontaneity of a process at constant T and P: ΔG < 0 spontaneous, ΔG = 0 equilibrium, ΔG > 0 non-spontaneous. For an ideal gas, ΔG = ΔG° + RT·ln Q, where Q is the reaction quotient. Temperature and pressure thus couple through G to dictate whether a chemical process will proceed, making Gibbs energy central to reactor design and gas-phase process control.
